params.text:
Further signs to watch for

As sepsis develops, other signs may appear. The person's heart may be beating very fast, and their skin may look mottled, pale, blotchy or bluish, especially on the lips, hands or feet. They may pass little or no urine over many hours. Some people describe an overwhelming feeling of impending doom — a sense that they are going to die — and this should always be taken seriously.

  • Skin: mottled, pale, blotchy or blue in colour.
  • Urine: passing much less than usual, or none at all.
  • Feeling: a strong sense that something is terribly wrong.

Putting the signs together

A helpful way to remember the adult warning signs is to think about slurred speech or confusion, extreme shivering or muscle pain, passing no urine, severe breathlessness, a feeling that they are going to die, and skin that is mottled or discoloured. Any one of these in an unwell person means you should act fast and get help.

Remember that a rash which does not fade when you press a glass against it is a separate medical emergency in its own right. If you see a non-fading rash, or any of the signs above, do not wait — escalate immediately and be ready to call 999. It is always better to raise the alarm and be reassured than to hold back and lose precious time.
